About AI-made code commits (I will not comment on the sloppy PR descriptions today 😅): They learned well that commits must be short and well commented to get approved. But the comments are basically always commenting on the obvious things in the commit that are self-explanatory, and not on the "decisions". And they invariably all use the same "good boy" approach (declare things, then implement) even when there would be ways to do without. So these commits are mostly not short but pure bloat.

I’m intrigued how many he can do in a day since the inspection is supposed to take 20 minutes. How does he replenish the drivers?
